data.title: Dow Gains Over 100 Points; Steel Dynamics Posts Downbeat Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stocks traded higher this morning, with the Dow Jones index gaining more than 100 points on Monday.

Following the market opening Monday, the Dow traded up 0.33% to 49,259.65 while the NASDAQ gained 0.09% to 23,521.63. The S&P 500 also rose, gaining, 0.26% to 6,933.84.

Check This Out: How To Earn $500 A Month From Goldman Sachs Stock Ahead Of Q4 Earnings

Leading and Lagging Sectors

Materials shares gained by 0.8% on Monday.

In trading on Monday, health care stocks dipped by 0.2%.

Top Headline

Steel Dynamics Inc (NASDAQ:STLD) reported downbeat earnings for the fourth quarter on Monday.

The company posted quarterly earnings of $1.82 per share which missed the analyst consensus estimate of $1.95 per share. The company reported quarterly sales of $4.414 billion which missed the analyst consensus estimate of $4.575 billion.

Equities Trading UP
           

  • Battalion Oil Corp (NYSE:BATL) shares shot up 379% to $6.13 after the company switched its gas processing partner and boosted production.
  • Shares of Brand Engagement Network Inc (NASDAQ:BNAI) got a boost, surging 196% to $48.79 following a strategic partnership announcement that has reinvigorated investor interest.
  • Gaxos.ai Inc. (NASDAQ:GXAI) shares were also up, gaining 76% to $1.97.

Equities Trading DOWN

  • Mingteng International Corp Inc (NASDAQ:MTEN) shares dropped 36% to $2.16.
  • Shares of CN ENERGY GROUP Inc (NASDAQ:CNEY) were down 35% to $0.52 after the company received Nasdaq Delisting determination for minimum bid price deficiency.
  • 60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals Inc (NASDAQ:SXTP) was down, falling 23% to $3.52 after dipping 10% on Friday.

Commodities

In commodity news, oil traded down 0.6% to $60.72 while gold traded up 1.8% at $5,068.50.

Silver traded up 8.1% to $109.565 on Monday, while copper rose 0.5% to $5.9770.

Euro zone

European shares were mixed today. The eurozone's STOXX 600 fell 0.1%, while Spain's IBEX 35 Index rose 0.4%. London's FTSE 100 rose 0.1%, Germany's DAX fell 0.1% and France's CAC 40 fell 0.2% during the session.

Asia Pacific Markets

Asian markets closed mixed on Monday, with Japan's Nikkei falling 1.79%, Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gaining 0.06% and China's Shanghai Composite declining 0.09%.

Economics

  • The Chicago Fed National Activity Index climbed to -0.04 in November from a reading of -0.42 in October.
  • U.S. durable-goods orders rose 5.3% month-over-month in November following a revised 2.1% plunge in October and topping market estimates of a 3.7% increase.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: UnitedHealth Earnings Are Imminent; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (NYSE:UNH) will release earnings results for the fourth quarter, before the opening bell on Tuesday, Jan. 27.

Analysts expect the Eden Prairie, Minnesota-based company to report quarterly earnings at $2.11 per share, down from $6.81 per share in the year-ago period. The consensus estimate for UnitedHealth's quarterly revenue is $113.73 billion, versus $100.81 billion a year earlier, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Stephen Hemsley, chairman and CEO of UnitedHealth Group, recently told lawmakers that rising hospital prices, consolidation, specialty services, and prescription drug costs—not insurers—are the primary forces pushing U.S. health care spending higher. He urged policy reforms to improve affordability and access.

UnitedHealth shares gained 0.5% to close at $356.26 on Friday.

Benzinga readers can access the latest analyst ratings on the Analyst Stock Ratings page. Readers can sort by stock ticker, company name, analyst firm, rating change or other variables.

Let's have a look at how Benzinga's most-accurate analysts have rated the company in the recent period.

  • Morgan Stanley analyst Ricky Goldwasser maintained an Overweight rating and cut the price target from $411 to $409 on Jan. 23, 2026. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 69%.
  • Barclays analyst Andrew Mok maintained the stock with an Overweight rating and raised the price target from $386 to $391 on Jan. 5, 2026. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 60%.
  • Bernstein analyst Lance Wilkes maintained the stock with an Outperform and raised the price target from $433 to $440 on Oct. 30, 2025. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 57%.
  • UBS analyst Kevin Caliendo maintained the stock with a Buy rating and raised the price target from $378 to $430 on Oct. 29, 2025. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 71%.
  • RBC Capital analyst Ben Hendrix maintained the stock with an Outperform rating and raised the price target from $286 to $408 on Oct. 29, 2025. This analyst has an accuracy rate of 61%.

data.title: Why Realty Income Sits Several Notches Above The BBB Cliff—And Why That Cliff Still Matters
data.description:
Realty Income Corporation (NYSE:O) has been trading around the low-$60s lately. The dividend yield sits in the mid-5% range as of late January 2026.

Credit ratings matter here. Realty Income carries an A- issuer rating from S&P Global Ratings and an A3 from Moody's Investors Service. Both have stable outlooks.

That rating isn't a comfort blanket. It's distance. Distance from the kind of downgrade that can turn an ordinary selloff into something mechanical.

Most income investors assume selling starts with a change of mind. Sometimes it does. In credit, it can start with something colder: rules. Capital requirements. Mandates. Internal guidelines. Constraints that don't care about the narrative.

That forced-supply dynamic is worth understanding. Even if you own a REIT that looks comfortably above the line.

The Regulatory Cliff

When a bond slips from investment grade (BBB-) into high yield (BB+), insurance companies don't always get to "wait it out." Capital rules can force the issue.

Under the NAIC Risk-Based Capital framework, required capital can rise as credit quality deteriorates. Using the 2021 revised C-1 bond factors for life insurers as an illustrative reference:

  • BBB- bond (NAIC 2.C): 2.168% capital charge
  • BB+ bond (NAIC 3.A): 3.151% capital charge

That's roughly a 45% increase.

Put differently: the same position can suddenly cost more capital. For an insurer, that's not a footnote. It's a constraint.

Many institutions respond the same way. They rebalance.

This is where retail framing often breaks. Retail sees price and yield. Institutions see a rulebook. If a holding becomes more expensive to carry, the question shifts. It becomes less "Do we like this issuer?" and more "Do we still qualify to own it?"

The first time you watch a downgrade trade in real time, it can look irrational—until you read the mandate.

When enough holders share the same constraint, selling can look like a fundamentals verdict. But it can behave like plumbing.

(Source: NAIC RBC instructions / 2021 revised C-1 factors for life insurers. Actual charges vary by insurer, portfolio composition, and treatment.)

Where Realty Income Stands

Realty Income sits in a different neighborhood.

At the A-/A3 level, it generally falls into an NAIC 1-type category. The capital burden is meaningfully lower than BBB- debt. That gap matters. It can support a steadier institutional bid during stress.

The operating profile helps explain the rating. In the company's Q3 2025 materials and investor presentation, Realty Income reported:

  • Occupancy: 98.7%
  • Net Debt / EBITDA: 5.4x
  • Fixed Charge Coverage: 4.7x
  • Total Liquidity: $3.5 billion

You don't need to memorize every metric. The direction is the point. These figures signal room. Room to refinance. Room to absorb higher rates. Room to avoid living on the BBB edge.

The company has also been active on financing. In early January 2026, Realty Income priced $750 million of convertible senior notes due 2029 (with an additional purchaser option disclosed). That's not a magic shield. But it is consistent with a company that treats capital access as a core part of the model.

None of this makes Realty Income immune to volatility. REITs move with rates. They move with spreads. They move with sentiment.

The point is narrower. The BBB-cliff forced-selling mechanism is not sitting directly under this name today.

The 2026 Refinancing Wall

The backdrop still matters. REITs have a meaningful maturity calendar ahead. Refinancing math is harder when rates and spreads are less forgiving than they were a few years ago.

Many borrowers have used "extend and amend" strategies. It buys time. It also pushes pressure forward. Less pain now. More pressure later.

For REITs sitting at BBB or BBB-, risk can build quietly. Interest expense rises at refinancing. Coverage compresses. Options narrow. Then language shifts. Outlook turns negative. Watchlists appear. The market starts pricing a different probability tree.

If that process ends in a downgrade through the investment-grade boundary, repositioning can get disorderly. Not because properties changed overnight. Because the investor base did.

The buyer pool shrinks. Rule-bound holders step away. The remaining bid demands a higher spread.

That's the cliff. It's not a metaphor. It's a change in who is allowed to stay.

Three Warning Signs To Watch (For Any BBB REIT)

If you hold BBB-adjacent REIT exposure elsewhere, these signals often show up before the downgrade conversation goes mainstream:

  1. Interest coverage drifting toward ~2.5x — trend matters as much as level
  2. Outlook shifts to negative — often creates a 6–18 month warning window
  3. Refinancing at meaningfully higher rates — stress usually starts in the debt stack

This isn't a prediction engine. It's a checklist. The goal isn't to call the top. It's to avoid being surprised by a change in constraints.

The Disconnect

Here's the pattern: when credit stress shows up, retail sees higher yield and assumes "better value." Institutions see forced supply.

That distinction matters. The selling isn't always driven by a new opinion about tenants or cap rates. It can be driven by mandates and capital rules. Market structure can overwhelm fundamentals in the short run.

By the time retail investors recognize the flow, much of the constrained base may already have moved. And once those holders exit, it can take time for a new buyer base to form.

The Bottom Line

Realty Income's A- rating provides meaningful buffer from the BBB cliff. That doesn't make the stock immune to volatility. It means forced selling is not the primary risk sitting directly underneath this name today.

But the framework matters. If you're reaching for yield in BBB-rated REITs elsewhere, you may be closer to a structural cliff than the yield alone suggests.

Distance is an asset in credit.

Next catalyst: Realty Income will report fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results after market close on February 24, 2026, followed by its investor call.

Disclosure: The author holds no positions in Realty Income Corporation (O) and has no plans to initiate any positions within 72 hours of publication. This article reflects the author's own analysis and opinions and is not investment advice. The author was not compensated by any third party for this article. NAIC capital charge figures reference the 2021 revised C-1 factors for life insurers; actual charges may vary. Financial metrics are sourced from Realty Income's Q3 2025 earnings materials, investor presentation, and recent debt financing disclosures.

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: USA Rare Earth Stock Pops On CHIPS Act LOI, Private Financing
data.description:
USA Rare Earth, Inc. (NASDAQ:USAR) shares are trading higher Monday after the company announced a non-binding letter of intent with the U.S. government for access to $1.6 billion in funding to accelerate the domestic rare earth value chain.

CHIPS Act Support, Private Capital Investment

USA Rare Earth said the letter of intent with the Department of Commerce's CHIPS Program includes $277 million in proposed federal funding and a proposed $1.3 billion senior secured loan under the CHIPS Act. The LOI is subject to further diligence, final agreements, customary closing conditions and approvals.

The company also announced a $1.5 billion common stock PIPE transaction anchored by Inflection Point, with participation from large mutual fund complexes. The PIPE consists of 69.8 million shares issued at $21.50 per share and is expected to close on Jan. 28, subject to customary closing conditions.

Together, the proposed government funding and private-sector investment would provide approximately $3.1 billion of capital.

USA Rare Earth said the capital is expected to accelerate and de-risk its growth plans across mining, processing, metal-making and magnet manufacturing. By 2030, the company aims to extract 40,000 metric tons per day of rare earth and critical mineral feedstock from its Round Top deposit, process 8,000 metric tons per year of heavy rare earth and critical mineral oxides and concentrates, reshore 10,000 metric tons per year of heavy rare earth metal and alloy-making capacity, expand neodymium-iron-boron magnet production capacity to 10,000 metric tons per year and process 2,000 metric tons per year of magnet production swarf.

In addition, USA Rare Earth said it entered into a collaboration with the U.S. Department of Energy's National Energy Technology Laboratory to advance heavy rare earth separation technologies using digital twin technology at its Wheat Ridge lab and Round Top deposit.

Technical Picture Of USA Rare Earth

Currently, USA Rare Earth is trading at $28.02, which positions it 68.7% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 56.5% above its 100-day SMA, indicating strong short-term momentum. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 33.53% and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, reflecting a robust upward trend.

The RSI is at 74.50, indicating that the stock is in overbought territory, which may suggest a potential pullback. Meanwhile, the MACD is above its signal line, reinforcing a bullish outlook for the stock.

The combination of an overbought RSI and a bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while the stock has been strong, caution may be warranted for potential corrections.

USAR Price Action: At the time of writing, USA Rare Earth stock is trading 19.50% higher at $29.88, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock
